title: DraftRestoreBanner — Recover Unsaved Cuts After Browser Crash
problem: If a user's browser crashes or the page reloads mid-edit, all cut regions are lost — the editor opens blank. The user may not realise their work was saved in localStorage and abandon the session assuming the work is gone.
analysis:
DraftRestoreBanner appears at the top of the editor when two conditions are true:
1. `hasDraft` from useCutDraft (localStorage key `odio:draft:{clipId}` exists and has content)
2. `cutMarks.length === 0` (editor currently has no cuts — so draft isn't already loaded)

When both are true: shows a banner — 'You have unsaved edits from a previous session.' with two buttons: 'Resume editing' (loads saved cuts into editor) and 'Dismiss' (clears localStorage draft).

Once the user taps 'Resume' or 'Dismiss', banner unmounts and doesn't reappear (hasDraft becomes false).
successCriteria:
- Banner visible only when hasDraft=true AND cutMarks.length === 0
- 'Resume editing' button: calls onResume() which loads draft cutMarks into WaveformEditor state
- 'Dismiss' button: calls onDismiss() which clears localStorage draft + hasDraft becomes false
- Banner does not appear when editor already has cuts (user navigated back with live state)
- Banner does not reappear after dismissal in same session
implementation: components/clips/DraftRestoreBanner.tsx: props { show: boolean, onResume: () => void, onDismiss: () => void }. show = hasDraft && cutMarks.length === 0 (computed in WaveformEditor). Renders an amber-bordered info banner with two buttons. Returns null when show=false.

decisions.rationale: If the editor already has cuts (e.g., user navigated Edit from a version that had cuts), the draft is irrelevant — the current state IS the working set. Showing the draft banner when cuts already exist would confuse the user and potentially overwrite valid in-flight edits.
decisions.recommendation: WaveformEditor passes show = hasDraft && cutMarks.length === 0. DraftRestoreBanner is purely controlled.

knownLimitations: Draft key is per-clipId, not per-clipId+versionId — if the user edits a different version, the same draft key is used and may restore mismatched cuts
userGuide: If your browser closed unexpectedly while you were editing cuts, a banner appears when you return offering to restore your work. Tap 'Resume' to get your cuts back, or 'Dismiss' to start fresh.
